task_completion.description: Log in to the Rancher dashboard
task_completion.visual_evidence: Browser window showing the Rancher UI dashboard at https://localhost, indicating a successful login.

task_completion.description: Read the health check specification file
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor or terminal window displaying the contents of /home/ga/Desktop/health_check_spec.yaml.

task_completion.description: Navigate to the 'services' namespace in Rancher
task_completion.visual_evidence: Rancher UI showing the 'Workloads' or 'Deployments' page specifically filtered to or displaying the 'services' namespace.

task_completion.description: Update health probes for the deployments
task_completion.visual_evidence: Rancher UI showing the 'Edit Deployment' screen (either form view or YAML view) where health checks (Liveness, Readiness, or Startup probes) are being added or modified for the deployments.

task_completion.description: Ensure all pods reach a stable Running state
task_completion.visual_evidence: Rancher UI showing the deployments in the 'services' namespace with a green 'Active' status, or a terminal showing 'Running' status with all containers ready (e.g., 1/1) for all pods in the namespace.

integrity.description: Verify the agent used the Rancher web interface
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent interacted with the Rancher web interface in a browser to perform the updates, aligning with the instruction to login to Rancher.

integrity.description: Verify the pod running state is genuine
integrity.visual_evidence: The final running state of the pods is displayed in the actual Rancher UI or standard kubectl output, not a manually typed text file or mocked terminal output.

integrity.description: Verify the agent saved the configuration changes
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the agent actually applying or saving the deployment configurations (e.g., clicking 'Save' in Rancher or applying YAML) rather than just viewing the edit screens.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The target namespace in Rancher is 'services'. The health probe specification file is located at '/home/ga/Desktop/health_check_spec.yaml'.
